summary.intent: Add Fireworks.ai as a primary provider in the moltis project, including ENV variable support, model detection/catalog, config template, and onboarding UI registration.
summary.outcome: Fireworks.ai was fully integrated across 4 files with a fallback model catalog (7 models including Kimi K2.5 Turbo as default), OpenAI-compatible API wiring, config template section, validation support, and a passing test. Changes were committed and ready to push/PR.

repo: New OpenAI-compatible providers require changes in exactly 4 places: crates/providers/src/lib.rs (catalog + OpenAiCompatDef), crates/config/src/validate.rs (KNOWN_PROVIDER_NAMES), crates/config/src/template.rs (config section + comment list), crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs (onboarding UI)
Fireworks.ai uses two distinct path prefixes for model IDs: accounts/fireworks/models/... for standard models and accounts/fireworks/routers/... for router/turbo variants
Existing providers like deepseek and cerebras have registration tests (e.g. deepseek_registers_with_api_key) that serve as the pattern for new provider tests
The clippy lint failure on the full workspace is a pre-existing cmake build issue in a dependency, not related to application code; running clippy scoped to changed crates is the correct check

code.finding: FIREWORKS_MODELS catalog added with 7 entries; Kimi K2.5 Turbo (accounts/fireworks/routers/kimi-k2p5-turbo) placed first as the fire-pass default
KNOWN_PROVIDER_NAMES array is where provider name strings must be registered for validation hint diagnostics
Two places need updating: the 'all available providers' comment list and the actual [providers.fireworks] config section
known_providers() function returns KnownProvider entries used by the onboarding/picker UI

workflow: Running cargo fmt separately after edits is necessary; the workspace formatter catches style issues that manual edits introduce
Scoping clippy to only changed crates (--package flag) avoids noise from pre-existing dependency issues
Beads issue tracking DB was unavailable during the session; implementation proceeded without creating a tracking issue

friction: Beads issue tracking DB was down — three consecutive attempts to create a tracking issue failed, requiring the assistant to skip that step and proceed directly to implementation

Fireworks.ai documentation required researching whether /v1/models endpoint was supported (it is, via /inference/v1/models) since the docs describe account-scoped endpoints which initially suggested standard discovery might not work
open_items: PR was committed and staged but the transcript ends before push and PR creation were confirmed complete
No test was added specifically for the Kimi K2.5 Turbo router model or the accounts/fireworks/routers/ path prefix variant
Beads tracking issue was never created due to DB downtime — should be filed when the DB is back online
